description_long: This object consists of several rectangular solar panels mounted on a patterned, flat base that resembles a section of rooftop or ground surface. The solar panels are dark, grid-patterned, and are arranged in two distinct tilted groups, each supported by visible mounting structures. The base is a large, flat, metallic or composite platform with a repeating geometric texture, likely representing a stylized roof or ground installation. The solar panels are elevated above the base at an angle, optimizing their exposure to sunlight. The mounting structures appear to be metallic, providing stability and support for the panels. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present on the exterior surfaces.
description: A set of dark, grid-patterned solar panels mounted on a large, textured flat base, with panels arranged in two tilted groups on metallic supports.
description_view_0: Two groups of tilted solar panels are clearly visible, mounted on a patterned flat base, with the panel surfaces facing upward and to the left.
description_view_1: The panels are seen from a lower angle, emphasizing the tilt and the supporting structures beneath them, with the base extending horizontally.
description_view_2: A top-down perspective shows the rectangular arrangement of the panels and the full extent of the patterned base, with less emphasis on the tilt.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the panels is visible, showing the tilt direction and the separation between the two groups of panels on the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing upward and slightly to the left; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or ground-mounted on a flat, patterned base with metallic support frames, likely aluminum or steel.
attribute_array_configuration: Two separate clusters of solar panels, each with 2-3 panels, arranged in parallel rows on a single large base.
